title: NormalForm
summary: Encodes the Poincaré normal form g_+ = r^{-2}(dr^2 + h_r) at the level of abstract metric data.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Encodes the Poincaré normal form g_+ = r^{-2}(dr^2 + h_r) at the level of abstract metric data.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/NormalForm.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Symbolic Poincare normal-form data. Its mathematical data are: metric: PEMetric Data. u, v, w X; r: Geodesic Defining Function X metric.g Plus; h0: Metric Symbol X.boundary; h R: Real maps to Metric Symbol X.boundary; h R zero: h R 0 equals h0; dr Squared: Metric Symbol X.bulk; slice Metric: Metric Symbol X.bulk; slice Metric certificate: Prop; boundary metric certificate: Prop; g Plus normal form: metric.g Plus equals Metric Symbol.scale By Function the function sending x to r.r x to the power 2 ⁻¹ Metric Symbol.add dr Squared slice Metric.
The identity gPlus = r^(-2) (dr^2 + h_r). In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlus_eq_normalForm : NF.metric.gPlus = MetricSymbol.scaleByFunction (fun x => ((NF.r.r x) ^ 2)⁻¹) (MetricSymbol.add NF.drSquared NF.sliceMetric)

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
